what_it_means: Total number of videos processed in this run
% of videos where the predicted valence matched ground truth. Simple hit rate. Baseline (always neutral) = 33.3%.
Average F1 across all 3 classes equally. Balances precision and recall. More informative than accuracy when classes are equal-sized.
Agreement beyond chance. 0 = no better than random, 1 = perfect. Accounts for class imbalance in predictions.
Average precision across classes. Of all videos the model predicted as class X, how many actually were X?
Average recall across classes. Of all actual class X videos, how many did the model correctly detect?
F1 score specifically for the POSITIVE class. Low = model struggles to detect happy/positive faces.
F1 score specifically for the NEUTRAL class. Low = model confuses resting faces with emotions.
F1 score specifically for the NEGATIVE class. Low = model misses or over-predicts upset/sad faces.
AUC for positive vs rest. How well the model ranks actual positive videos higher than non-positive ones using score_positive. 0.5 = random, 1.0 = perfect.
AUC for neutral vs rest. How well the model ranks actual neutral videos higher using score_neutral.
AUC for negative vs rest. How well the model ranks actual negative videos higher using score_negative.
Average AUC across all 3 classes. Single headline confidence-ranking score. Above 0.7 = acceptable, above 0.8 = good.
Average processing time per video in milliseconds. Relevant for real-time robot deployment.
Median latency — half of videos processed faster than this.
95th percentile latency — worst-case speed for 95% of videos.
